notes: Three back-to-back no-op turns ('Reply with exactly the word: ok') in one gemini-cli session, stressing the MULTI-turn token accumulator (single-turn extraction is covered by basic-turn). Recorded with the INTERACTIVE gemini-cli driver (gemini -y under tmux: three send+wait_turn pairs, 8s trailing sleeps), NOT headless `gemini -p` (a one-shot run exits at turn completion, before the daemon can observe each working→ready edge). Each assistant 'gemini' message persists a per-message tokens block (input/output/cached/total); applyTokens emits a deduped-by-id PerTurnContribution per turn (Input=input-cached, CacheRead=cached, since gemini's input is cache-inclusive), and the shared applyContribution accumulates into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens/CumCacheReadTokens add-only across turns → TotalTokens > 0 after turn 1 and non-decreasing after turns 2/3. If a later turn fully hits the prompt cache, CacheReadTokens > 0 with the turn's net billed Input = 0. The phase DSL has no metric form, so these token invariants are asserted via the observations vector below (tokens_nonzero/cost_nonzero) plus the gemini-cli parser unit tests; the phases assert only the three-turn state arc (each turn_end anchored to its own turn_start so the matcher picks the post-turn ready). Gemini emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is inferred from a final text-only message with no open tool calls. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the basic-turn live-verified default and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it; a different live default is a string update, not a daemon bug.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Turn 1 completes back to ready (final text-only message, no tool calls); first PerTurnContribution flushed → TotalTokens > 0
Turn 2 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic)
Turn 3 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2 (monotonic accumulation across 3 turns); cached portion (if any) accrues to CumCacheReadTokens
